python3 << 'PYEOF'
import yfinance as yf

# Check market caps of potential candidates
candidates = [
    'HIMS', 'CART', 'IOT', 'CARG', 'RDFN', 'UPST', 'APPN', 'EVS',
    'SOUN', 'CVNA', 'DKNG', 'PATH', 'FTNT', 'OKTA', 'CELH', 'PLTR',
    'SNOW', 'DDOG', 'CRWD', 'APP', 'AFRM', 'OPEN', 'BILL', 'GTLB',
    'DOCS', 'ASAN', 'ZS', 'NET', 'ESTC', 'MDB', 'U', 'SNAP', 'PINS',
    'RBLX', 'ARM', 'S', 'AI', 'BBAI', 'MARA', 'RIOT', 'CLSK', 'BITF',
    'HIVE', 'CIFR', 'WULF', 'CORZ', 'NRDS', 'CVCO', 'MDC', 'LXU',
    'HAYN', 'CRMT', 'AAP', 'PFGC', 'HAIN', 'JJSF', 'CALM', 'SENEA',
    'UNFI', 'USFD', 'SPTN', 'GO', 'SFM', 'WING', 'BLMN', 'CAKE',
    'BBWI', 'URBN', 'AEO', 'GPS', 'ANF', 'HBI', 'PVH', 'RL',
    'KIM', 'BXP', 'VTR', 'O', 'STAG', 'REXR', 'CUBE', 'LSI',
    'NSA', 'COLD', 'TSP', 'EGP', 'FR', 'WPC', 'NNN', 'EPRT',
    'ADC', 'BRX', 'KRG', 'ROIC', 'SRC', 'EPST', 'PINE', 'AHH',
    'CHMI', 'CPT', 'EQR', 'AVB', 'ESS', 'MAA', 'UDR', 'HST',
    'PEAK', 'SHO', 'RHP', 'ABG', 'SUI', 'LTC', 'DOC', 'OHI',
    'HR', 'CTRE', 'LXP', 'WRI', 'REG',
]

results = []
for ticker in candidates:
    try:
        stock = yf.Ticker(ticker)
        info = stock.info
        mcap = info.get('marketCap', 0)
        name = info.get('shortName', '')
        if mcap and 1e9 <= mcap <= 10e9:
            results.append((ticker, name, mcap))
    except Exception as e:
        pass

for t, name, mcap in sorted(results, key=lambda x: x[2]):
    print(f"{t:8s} | {name:40s} | ${mcap/1e9:.2f}B")

import yfinance as yf
import json

stock = yf.Ticker('HIMS')
info = stock.info

# Save key info
print("=== KEY COMPANY INFO ===")
for key in ['shortName', 'longName', 'sector', 'industry', 'marketCap', 
            'currentPrice', 'fiftyTwoWeekHigh', 'fiftyTwoWeekLow',
            'trailingPE', 'forwardPE', 'priceToBook', 'totalRevenue',
            'revenuePerShare', 'grossMargins', 'operatingMargins',
            'profitMargins', 'returnOnAssets', 'returnOnEquity',
            'totalCash', 'totalDebt', 'totalCashPerShare',
            'debtToEquity', 'currentRatio', 'quickRatio',
            'earningsGrowth', 'revenueGrowth', 'grossProfits',
            'ebitda', 'operatingCashflow', 'freeCashflow',
            'dividendRate', 'dividendYield', 'payoutRatio',
            'beta', 'sharesOutstanding', 'floatShares',
            'shortRatio', 'shortPercentOfFloat',
            'bookValue', 'enterpriseValue', 'enterpriseToRevenue',
            'enterpriseToEbitda', '52WeekChange', 'SandP52WeekChange',
            'lastDividendValue', 'lastDividendDate']:
    val = info.get(key)
    if val is not None:
        print(f"{key}: {val}")

# Save to file
with open('/workspace/raw/hims_info.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(info, f, indent=2, default=str)
print("\nSaved to /workspace/raw/hims_info.json")

import yfinance as yf
import pandas as pd

stock = yf.Ticker('HIMS')

# Get financial statements
print("=== INCOME STATEMENT (Annual) ===")
income = stock.financials
print(income.to_string())

print("\n=== INCOME STATEMENT (Quarterly) ===")
income_q = stock.quarterly_financials
print(income_q.to_string())

print("\n=== BALANCE SHEET (Annual) ===")
balance = stock.balance_sheet
print(balance.to_string())

print("\n=== BALANCE SHEET (Quarterly) ===")
balance_q = stock.quarterly_balance_sheet
print(balance_q.to_string())

print("\n=== CASH FLOW (Annual) ===")
cashflow = stock.cashflow
print(cashflow.to_string())

print("\n=== CASH FLOW (Quarterly) ===")
cashflow_q = stock.quarterly_cashflow
print(cashflow_q.to_string())

# Save to CSV
income.to_csv('/workspace/raw/hims_income_annual.csv')
income_q.to_csv('/workspace/raw/hims_income_quarterly.csv')
balance.to_csv('/workspace/raw/hims_balance_annual.csv')
balance_q.to_csv('/workspace/raw/hims_balance_quarterly.csv')
cashflow.to_csv('/workspace/raw/hims_cashflow_annual.csv')
cashflow_q.to_csv('/workspace/raw/hims_cashflow_quarterly.csv')
print("\nSaved all financials to /workspace/raw/")

import requests
import json
import os
import hashlib
from datetime import datetime

# Track sources
sources = []

def log_source(url, filename, content_hash):
    sources.append({
        'url': url,
        'filename': filename,
        'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat(),
        'sha256': content_hash
    })

# Get HIMS SEC filings from EDGAR
ticker = 'HIMS'
# HIMS CIK: 0001811210
cik = '0001811210'

# Get filings index
url = f'https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar?action=getcompany&CIK={cik}&type=10-K&dateb=&owner=include&count=5&search_text=&action=getcompany'
headers = {'User-Agent': 'Investment Analyst (research@memo.com)'}

# Use the EDGAR full-text search API instead
url = f'https://data.sec.gov/submissions/CIK{cik}.json'
resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=30)
resp.raise_for_status()
data = resp.json()

# Save the raw index
with open('/workspace/raw/filings/sec_filings_index.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(data, f, indent=2)

content_hash = hashlib.sha256(resp.content).hexdigest()
log_source(url, 'sec_filings_index.json', content_hash)

print(f"Found {len(data.get('filings', {}).get('accessionNumbers', []))} filings")

# List recent filings
filings = data.get('filings', {})
recent = filings.get('recent', [])
for f in recent[:10]:
    print(f"  {f['form']} | {f['filingDate']} | {f['primaryDocumentDescription']}")

# Get 10-K filings
all_filings = filings.get('accessionNumbers', [])
forms_10k = []
forms_10q = []
forms_8k = []

for acc in all_filings:
    # Parse the accession number to get form type
    # Format: CIK0001811210/0001811210-24-000012/0001811210-24-000012-index.htm
    pass

# Let's get the filing details
for f in recent[:20]:
    form = f.get('form', '')
    date = f.get('filingDate', '')
    desc = f.get('primaryDocumentDescription', '')
    print(f"  {form:6s} | {date} | {desc}")

import requests
import json
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

headers = {'User-Agent': 'Investment Analyst (research@memo.com)'}

# Try the SEC company lookup API
# Hims & Hers Health - let's try different CIKs
# From yfinance, we can get the CIK
import yfinance as yf
stock = yf.Ticker('HIMS')
info = stock.info
print(f"CIK from yfinance: {info.get('cik', 'N/A')}")
print(f"Company name: {info.get('longName', 'N/A')}")

# Try to get CIK from the company info
# Let's also try the SEC's company search via the API
# The SEC has a company lookup at: https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar
# But let's try the direct data API with different CIKs

# Common CIKs for HIMS
for cik in ['0001811210', '0001811211', '0001811212', '0001811213', '0001811214', '0001811215']:
    url = f'https://data.sec.gov/submissions/CIK{cik}.json'
    resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=10)
    if resp.status_code == 200:
        data = resp.json()
        name = data.get('name', '')
        tickers = data.get('tickers', [])
        print(f"CIK {cik}: {name} | Tickers: {tickers}")
        if 'HIMS' in tickers or 'Hims' in name:
            print(f"  *** FOUND HIMS: CIK {cik} ***")
            break

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import requests
import json
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

headers = {'User-Agent': 'Investment Analyst (research@memo.com)'}

# Try the SEC's company search API
# The SEC has an API at https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar
# Let's try searching for "Hims"

# Method 1: Use the SEC's company search form
url = 'https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar'
params = {
    'action': 'company_search',
    'company_or_ticker': 'Hims'
}
resp = requests.get(url, params=params, headers=headers, timeout=30)
soup = BeautifulSoup(resp.text, 'html.parser')

# Look for CIK in the response
links = soup.find_all('a')
for link in links:
    href = link.get('href', '')
    text = link.get_text(strip=True)
    if 'CIK' in href or 'cik' in href.lower():
        print(f"Link: {text} -> {href}")

# Method 2: Try the SEC's full-text search
url2 = 'https://efts.sec.gov/LATEST/search-index?q=Hims+Hers&dateRange=custom&startDate=2024-01-01&endDate=2025-12-31&forms=10-K'
resp2 = requests.get(url2, headers=headers, timeout=30)
print(f"\nFull-text search status: {resp2.status_code}")

# Method 3: Try known CIK ranges for recent IPOs
# HIMS went public via SPAC in 2021
# Let's try CIKs in the 000181xxxx range
for cik_num in range(1811000, 1812000):
    cik = f"{cik_num:010d}"
    url = f'https://data.sec.gov/submissions/CIK{cik}.json'
    try:
        resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=5)
        if resp.status_code == 200:
            data = resp.json()
            name = data.get('name', '')
            tickers = data.get('tickers', [])
            if 'HIMS' in tickers or 'Hims' in name or 'Hers' in name:
                print(f"FOUND: CIK {cik}: {name} | Tickers: {tickers}")
                break
    except:
        pass
    if cik_num % 100 == 0:
        print(f"Checked up to CIK {cik}...")

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import requests
import json
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

headers = {'User-Agent': 'Investment Analyst (research@memo.com)'}

# Try the SEC's company search via the browse-edgar form
# The form action is getcompany with company name
url = 'https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar'
params = {
    'action': 'getcompany',
    'company': 'Hims & Hers Health',
    'type': '10-K',
    'dateb': '',
    'owner': 'include',
    'count': '5'
}
resp = requests.get(url, params=params, headers=headers, timeout=30)
soup = BeautifulSoup(resp.text, 'html.parser')

# Look for CIK references
text = soup.get_text()
# Find CIK numbers in the text
import re
ciks = re.findall(r'CIK=000\d{7,8}', text)
print(f"CIKs found in page: {ciks}")

# Also look for accession numbers
accessions = re.findall(r'000\d{8,9}-\d{2}-\d{4,6}', text)
print(f"Accessions found: {accessions[:10]}")

# Print relevant parts of the page
for line in text.split('\n'):
    if 'Hims' in line or 'Hers' in line or 'CIK' in line or '10-K' in line:
        print(line.strip()[:200])

# Print the structure

import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import re

headers = {'User-Agent': 'Investment Analyst (research@memo.com)'}
cik = '0001773751'

# Get the index page for the most recent 10-K
accession = '0001773751-26-000022'
index_url = f'https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/{cik}/{accession}/{accession}-index.htm'
resp = requests.get(index_url, headers=headers, timeout=30)
soup = BeautifulSoup(resp.text, 'html.parser')

# Find all links
print("=== ALL LINKS IN INDEX ===")
for a in soup.find_all('a'):
    href = a.get('href', '')
    text = a.get_text(strip=True)
    if href and not href.startswith('http'):
        print(f"  {text[:50]:50s} -> {href}")

# Look for the primary document (usually the HTML filing)
print("\n=== LOOKING FOR PRIMARY DOCUMENT ===")
for a in soup.find_all('a'):
    href = a.get('href', '')
    if '.htm' in href or '.html' in href:
        print(f"  Found: {href}")

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

headers = {'User-Agent': 'Investment Analyst (research@memo.com)'}
cik = '0001773751'

# Try the exact URL from the index page
accession = '0001773751-26-000022'
doc_name = 'hims-20251231.htm'

# Try different URL formats
urls_to_try = [
    f'https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/{cik}/{accession}/{doc_name}',
    f'https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/{cik}/{accession}/{doc_name}',
    f'https://www.sec.gov/ix?doc=/Archives/edgar/data/{cik}/{accession}/{doc_name}',
    f'https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/viewer?action=view&cik={cik}&accession_number={accession}&xbrl_type=v',
]

for url in urls_to_try:
    resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=30, allow_redirects=True)
    print(f"URL: {url[:80]}...")
    print(f"  Status: {resp.status_code}, Size: {len(resp.content)}, Final URL: {resp.url[:80]}")
    if resp.status_code == 200 and len(resp.content) > 1000:
        print(f"  Content preview: {resp.content[:200]}")
        break

# Also try with curl-like approach
print("\n--- Trying with different headers ---")
headers2 = {
    'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36',
    'Accept': 'text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8',
    'Accept-Language': 'en-US,en;q=0.5',
}

url = f'https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/{cik}/{accession}/{doc_name}'
resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers2, timeout=30)
print(f"Status: {resp.status_code}, Size: {len(resp.content)}")
if resp.status_code == 200:
    print(f"Content: {resp.content[:500]}")
PYEOF
